title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule

seo.title: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of baking an authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe at home. Get our step-by-step guide for that perfect tangy crust and airy crumb. Start baking today!
longTailKeywords: how to make San Francisco Sourdough Boule
easy San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
San Francisco Sourdough Boule step by step
best San Francisco Sourdough Boule at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: baking San Francisco sourdough at home
seo.h1: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
h2Headings: What is San Francisco Sourdough Boule?
Step-by-Step Instructions for San Francisco Sourdough
Pro Tips for the Best Sourdough Boule
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked San Francisco Sourdough Boule with a golden, crackled crust and an open, airy crumb.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the iconic taste of San Francisco with this homemade sourdough boule – perfect for any meal!

description: Experience the iconic taste of San Francisco with this homemade sourdough boule. This recipe guides you to achieve that perfectly tangy flavor, a wonderfully crisp crust, and an open, airy crumb. Enjoy the satisfaction of baking a true American classic right in your own kitchen.

instructions.title: Activate Your Starter
instructions.text: Feed your sourdough starter 4-6 hours before you plan to mix your dough, ensuring it is active and bubbly, floating when a small piece is dropped in water.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the active sourdough starter and warm water. Stir until the starter is mostly dissolved. Add the bread flour and salt, mixing with your hands or a Danish dough whisk until no dry flour remains. Cover the bowl and let it rest for 30 minutes for the autolyse.

instructions.title: Bulk Fermentation with Stretch and Folds
instructions.text: Over the next 3-4 hours, perform 3-4 sets of stretch and folds every 30-45 minutes. To do this, wet your hands, grab a portion of the dough, stretch it upwards, and fold it over itself. Rotate the bowl and repeat until you have gone around the entire dough. Cover and let it rest between sets.

instructions.title: Shape the Boule
instructions.text: Gently turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Preshape it into a round, letting it rest for 20 minutes to relax the gluten. Then, perform a final shaping into a tight boule, creating tension on the surface for a good crust.

instructions.title: Cold Proof
instructions.text: Place the shaped boule seam-side up into a floured banneton or a bowl lined with a floured kitchen towel. Cover tightly with plastic wrap to prevent drying and refrigerate for 12-18 hours for the cold proof. This develops flavor and makes it easier to handle.

instructions.title: Preheat Oven and Dutch Oven
instructions.text: About 30-45 minutes before baking, preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C) with a Dutch oven inside. Ensure it is fully preheated to get that initial burst of steam.

instructions.title: Bake the Sourdough
instructions.text: Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven. Invert the dough into it, score the top with a sharp lame or razor blade, and bake covered for 20 minutes. The lid traps steam, creating a crisp crust.

instructions.title: Finish Baking and Cool
instructions.text: Remove the lid from the Dutch oven, reduce the oven temperature to 450°F (230°C), and continue baking for another 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is deep golden brown and well-caramelized. Transfer the baked boule to a wire rack and let it cool completely for at least 1-2 hours before slicing for best results.

tips: For a truly authentic San Francisco tang, ensure your sourdough starter is very active and has a robust sour aroma before mixing. A longer cold proof also deepens the flavor profile.
Always use high-quality bread flour for the best gluten development and crumb structure. Avoid using all-purpose flour unless specifically adapted for lower protein content.
Store your cooled sourdough boule at room temperature, cut-side down on a cutting board, or in a bread bag for up to 3-4 days. It freezes beautifully when sliced for longer storage.
article: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a beloved American Baking dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
